intro: The clearest gap in the data is developer experience: Phosphor Icons sits at 5/5, Feather Icons at 4/5. Teams feel that gap day-to-day. Phosphor Icons is built for brands wanting expressive weight variation across web, mobile, and flutter products and is not a fit for ultra-minimal uis or projects where single-weight consistency is the goal. Feather Icons is built for legacy projects already using feather or fans of the minimalist single-weight style and is not a fit for new projects needing active updates, more icons, or multiple weights. On the scoring side, Phosphor Icons rates 5/5 for developer experience and 5/5 for transparency; Feather Icons rates 4/5 and 5/5 respectively. The honest trade-off: with Phosphor Icons you accept that larger catalog than needed for simple uis, while Feather Icons comes with the reality that maintenance has been slow for years. Neither side is free. Phosphor Icons's headline strength in the data is that 1200+ icons across six weights including duotone and fill.
quick_take: Phosphor Icons is for brands wanting expressive weight variation across web, mobile, and flutter products; Feather Icons is for legacy projects already using feather or fans of the minimalist single-weight.
verdict_a: Choose Phosphor Icons if you are brands wanting expressive weight variation across web, mobile, and flutter products and developer ergonomics (5/5) matter more than niche fit.
verdict_b: Choose Feather Icons if you are legacy projects already using feather or fans of the minimalist single-weight style and its workflow specificity outweighs a 4/5 DX rating.

faq.q: Is Phosphor Icons cheaper than Feather Icons?
faq.a: Both list a free pricing model in our data, and both publish a free tier. We don't have per-seat numbers in this dataset, so 'cheaper' depends on volume. Price both on your actual usage before deciding.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Phosphor Icons to Feather Icons?
faq.a: Phosphor Icons has low lock-in (open source icon library, free to use); Feather Icons has low lock-in (oss svg icon set). Migration effort scales with how much state and automation you've built in Phosphor Icons; plan for data export, config rebuild, and a parallel-run period before cutting over.

In our data, Phosphor Icons scores 5/5 for developer experience versus 4/5 for Feather Icons. That said, DX is subjective; the Feather Icons score may still be acceptable if its feature fit is stronger for your use case.

faq.q: Is Feather Icons a good alternative to Phosphor Icons?
faq.a: Feather Icons is built for legacy projects already using feather or fans of the minimalist single-weight style and explicitly not for new projects needing active updates, more icons, or multiple weights, so it's a fit only if your workflow matches its stated audience. If you were using Phosphor Icons for brands wanting expressive weight variation across web, mobile, and flutter products, the switch is reasonable; outside that scope, look elsewhere.
